It is important that all of us verify NetSuite before posting numbers on the report and the whiteboard to help avoid issues with the orders.

The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

The flow wrap is checked and looks good, with well-sealed edges and a good appearance. The lot number is legible and printed in the location required by the spec.

At the start of the second-shift production run, the production team detected an unusual sound in the C6 conveyor belt area. Upon conducting the corresponding inspection, a screw was observed rubbing against the roller's plastic guard.


During the investigation, metal particles and dust were identified in the affected zone; consequently, immediate steps were taken to clean and sanitize the area in order to eliminate any potential foreign material residue.


The line will continue to operate under constant monitoring throughout the shift, with the objective of verifying the effectiveness of the implemented corrective actions and ensuring that production remains unaffected.
Assembled. QA will do inspection then we can start
Line is running

We have problem with Cavanna flow wrap the film keep coming out open. 





We try putting new roll and see if there is any changes.
Demolding, metal detector and C6 belts are running correctly
Last hour was bad because we kept getting jams on the infeed to cavanna. We cleaned the rails but the issue ended up being the sensor not reading correctly it’s been fixed and is running better
Demolding, metal detector and C6 belts are running correctly
Current Flow Wrap Inspection

Molding Line 1 has experienced certain deviations and issues, including irregular chocolate pieces lacking a uniform base, as well as the presence of curvature and deformations—issues that have been reported to Production for corrective action. Additionally, the most recent inspection revealed minor sealing defects affecting the product's presentation; consequently, the matter was addressed, and adjustments were implemented to ensure proper sealing. Regarding other areas—specifically tempering, weighing, and palletizing—operations are proceeding and being monitored within established parameters. The C6 conveyor belt area is subject to constant inspection, and no foreign material has been detected.
Demolding, metal detector and C6 belts are running correctly
A product verification is conducted at the start of the shift; the weight, organoleptic properties, flow wrap, and final packaging—as well as the final label—are found to be correct.

What adjustments are we having to make?

The countdown board had an error today. After reviewing the information with Fredy Montalvanwe found there was an extra 47,127 pieces added that were not produced. The countdown board has now been corrected. Raoul Lubain Nathan Gevenois

Imperial,
What do you think the problem is?

We are encountering an inconsistency in the chocolate regarding the product's required appearance; however, the team lead has been notified, and there is currently someone on-site removing the product that exhibits this poor appearance.
In my opinion, the molds lose their constant temperature because the line stops; the chocolate may also be slightly cold, and that, too, could be a contributing factor Robert Villarreal

Given the situation with starting and stopping, I agree 👍
 
If the chocolate is cold you can confirm with the temp gun when you start but otherwise continue to monitor mold temps

I have checked the temperature of the chocolate, and it is at 85. I will continue monitoring the mold's temperature and try to keep it constant between 84 and 85 to see if the chocolate's appearance improves.

The WDS speed should automatically change TG speed. I will ask Felix tomorrow to understand better.

Cnc ran well last time, so there may not be any auto rejections in place. The good thing is there is only 2 deposits, so it will be easier to determine how many we want to reject. 
 
Tyson Fausett next time we have an extended stop, have your 1st depositor operator mark the first 5 molds after deposit. Then we can evaluate at exit how many need to be auto rejected.
 
We probably want to do the same exercise of 5 molds after 2nd deposit if the line stops. I will ask for a blanket program that covers more than we need, but it would be good to narrow it down.
He said it isn’t working. So that’s why we are having to manually change it
What is working at this point then..🤬

Update from third shift: Product meets all specifications. Bar weight and depth dimensions are at target values. Lot placement for film has been correctly. Packaging is following all specifications. Correct lot is printed on both the display/film. Silver part on the back of the film is below 3mm. The film packaging is centered. Production is using the correct shipping box label. No issues to report.

Rose Smuin Chad Stone Kimberly Morales We are currently managing TG issues while only running TG5 and Tag 4. I have Anthony stationed at the demolding area monitoring the Gondola percentage. When the Gondola reaches 90% capacity, he slows the line down until another TG is up and running, then increases the line speed again. This process is helping us minimize scrap. Previously, when the Gondola reached 98% capacity and TG3 or TG4 was not running, all product was automatically diverted to scrap. TG issues are minor TG 5 is having lot code issues with time wont change
Maintenance is currently working on the issue
TG5 lot code time was fixed and is working properly

We're going to take a 20-minutes pause. We've run out of the corn syrup we were using; we're going to switch to the cold supply and see how long it takes to load a batch.
We are going to start running again. We have been able to raise the caramel level in the reserve kettle, and it appears there are no issues loading the corn syrup.

We tried with a new Stainless Steel from 2.0 and successfully passed

QA was seeing the same issue on the x-rays for MM4 & MM5, so they brought a different Stainless Steel test card which has been rejected every pass. Nothing will need to be placed on hold or reworked. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
We replaced the SS card without changing the threshold/slope, and it was detected and rejected every single time.

MM5 is not recognizing Non Ferrous 2mm card, already tried 10 times now and failed twice. What could be the best plan considering the past issue with MM3? Angelica Perez 
Charity Young
Angelica, do we have a new NF card? And do we have records of when the cards are replaced?
So are we putting product on hold?

Dakota and I are discussing the situation with Victor and Angelica. It doesn't make sense to me to put product from MM3 on hold when we've established that the x-ray is functioning correctly. I want to test the x-ray for MM5 with a different non-ferrous card before deciding to put product on hold.

At this point we should put the production of the MM3 and MM5 on hold, since your last successful verification William Beattie Victor Mejia We will work with maintenance to investigate further what is happening
I need to check to see if we have any new ones. The cards that have failed are taken out of service, set aside, and stored away so they won't be used again.
I also don’t want use to continue to make product if it will be put on hold

So we decided, on MM3 everything from 6:20pm to 7pm and MM5 from 6:50pm to 7:10pm needs to be reworked due to X-ray failure.
Charity Young 
Angelica Perez 
Dakota Adair

The issue with the MM’s not reading the card is fixed.

The issue was figuring out the time line of what we had to rework and the disconnect of how MM5 not reading a non-Ferris card would result in MM3 also getting placed on hold.

We are going to pass the pallets through the Xray machine again so there will be nothing on hold it would be rework.

After checking times of the pallets, we will rework 3 pallets from MM3 and just 1 from MM5. Thanks for the support guys
Angelica Perez

We tried with a new card of the same Standard range 2.0 and were rejected on all 3 x-rays. The failed card will be suspended; I agree that we shouldn't put anything on hold unless you disagree. 178 bags of scrap by 9:00 AM. I decided to shut down Matrix 4; 80% of the output had to be reopened because the silver content was too obvious. Consequently, we had less scrap by 9:00 AM; we are currently working only with Matrix 3 and 5. Nathan Topham Justin Garner
The MD belt suddenly stopped, and we are not able to start it back, even after resetting it. Maintenance is helping us troubleshoot. The line was paused while we find the root cause. Justin

The MD belt is now going after doing two hard resets of 30 and 40 seconds. Q.A. tech is currently passing the cards to make sure the MD is properly rejecting them, and then we will start back up. The root cause of the problem is still unknown.
